If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.","enum":["ASC","DESC"],"type":"string"},"labels":{"description":"Filter by labels","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"orderBy":{"description":"Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.","enum":["CREATED_AT","UPDATED_AT","COMMENTS"],"type":"string"},"owner":{"description":"Repository owner","type":"string"},"perPage":{"description":"Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)","maximum":100,"minimum":1,"type":"number"},"repo":{"description":"Repository name","type":"string"},"since":{"description":"Filter by date (ISO 8601 timestamp)","type":"string"},"state":{"description":"Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided","enum":["OPEN","CLOSED"],"type":"string"}},"required":["owner","repo"],"type":"object"},"name":"list_issues"},{"annotations":{"readOnlyHint":true,"title":"Search issues"},"description":"Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue","icons":[{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"light"},{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"dark"}],"inputSchema":{"properties":{"order":{"description":"Sort order","enum":["asc","desc"],"type":"string"},"owner":{"description":"Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.","type":"string"},"page":{"description":"Page number for pagination (min 1)","minimum":1,"type":"number"},"perPage":{"description":"Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)","maximum":100,"minimum":1,"type":"number"},"query":{"description":"Search query using GitHub issues search syntax","type":"string"},"repo":{"description":"Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.","type":"string"},"sort":{"description":"Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match","enum":["comments","reactions","reactions-+1","reactions--1","reactions-smile","reactions-thinking_face","reactions-heart","reactions-tada","interactions","created","updated"],"type":"string"}},"required":["query"],"type":"object"},"name":"search_issues"}]}}}

2026-04-09T02:59:47.7818107Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-09T02:57:02.399347376Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57971,\"title\":\"[GeneratorAgent] Docs: Document `build_and_classify` return type and `generatedGuard` field in README\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.GeneratorAgent`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/tools/Azure.GeneratorAgent/`\\n**Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (PR #57957) by `@Copilot`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #57957 documented the `TestResult` structured return type for the `run_tests` tool in README.md and CHANGELOG.md, by adding property names (`Success`, `ExitCode`, `Passed`, `Failed`, `Skipped`, `Total`, `Failures`, `Error`, `RawOutput`) inline in the MCP Tools table. This established a pattern of documenting tool return types in the table.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The `build_and_classify` tool\\u0026#39;s response shape is **not described** in the README — yet it returns a rich JSON object with 9 fields (including an optional `generatedGuard` sub-object), which is equally important for agents calling the tool to understand\\n- The automatic `generatedGuard` behaviour — when a Generated/ snapshot exists, `build_and_classify` auto-verifies integrity and reverts any violations, returning the result in the `generatedGuard` field — is completely absent from the README\\n- The `classify_errors` tool\\u0026#39;s inputs and response are also undescribed; it classifies a batch of raw build errors rather than running a build itself, but there\\u0026#39;s no indication of what JSON it returns\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/tools/Azure.GeneratorAgent/README.md` — **edit**\\n\\nIn the MCP Tools table, update the `build_and_classify` row from:\\n\\n```markdown\\n| `build_and_classify` | Run `dotnet build`, parse output, and classify each error as deterministic or requires-reasoning |\\n```\\n\\nto:\\n\\n```markdown\\n| `build_and_classify` | Run `dotnet build`, parse output, and classify each error as deterministic or requires-reasoning. Returns `success`, `exitCode`, `totalErrors`, `returnedErrors` (capped at 20), `deterministicCount`, `requiresReasoningCount`, `classifiedErrors` (list of classified error objects), `rawOutput`, optional `buildFailureHint` (when errors couldn\\u0026#39;t be parsed), and optional `generatedGuard` (when a Generated/ snapshot exists — auto-verifies integrity and reverts any violations). |\\n```\\n\\nUpdate the `classify_errors` row from:\\n\\n```markdown\\n| `classify_errors` | Classify a batch of build errors against the deterministic fix registry |\\n```\\n\\nto:\\n\\n```markdown\\n| `classify_errors` | Classify a batch of pre-parsed build errors against the deterministic fix registry without running a build. Returns a list of classified error objects, each with `errorCode`, `message`, `filePath`, `line`, `isDeterministic`, `toolName`, and `toolArgs`. |\\n```\\n\\nAlso add a new sub-section under **Key concepts** → after the MCP Tools table, to document the `generatedGuard` automatic behaviour:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Generated code integrity guard\\n\\nWhen `build_and_classify` is called and a Generated/ snapshot exists (taken via `snapshot_generated`), the tool automatically verifies that no files in `Generated/` were modified during the build-fix cycle. If violations are found, they are auto-reverted and the `generatedGuard` field in the response will contain:\\n\\n- `violated` — `true` if violations were detected\\n- `message` — human-readable summary\\n- `violations` — list of `{ relativePath, type }` objects describing each modified or added file\\n\\nThis guard prevents accidental edits to generated code and runs transparently on every `build_and_classify` call after a snapshot is taken.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNo snippet test files are needed for this documentation change. The README uses prose and table descriptions (not `#region Snippet:` blocks) to document the MCP tools.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README already contains all required sections (Getting started, Key concepts, Examples, Troubleshooting, Next steps, Contributing). This change adds content within the existing Key concepts section only — no sections need to be added.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/tools/Azure.GeneratorAgent/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/tools/Azure.GeneratorAgent/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `dotnet format sdk/tools/Azure.GeneratorAgent/`\\n\\nNo snippet update or API export is needed since this is a tool package with no public API surface changes.\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24169696229/agentic_workflow) · ● 657.2K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22\\u0026amp;type=issues)\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57971\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"github-actions[bot]\",\"id\":41898282,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/apps/github-actions\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"NONE\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":0,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":0},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-09T02:53:52Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-09T02:53:52Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

This established a pattern of documenting tool return types in the table.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The `build_and_classify` tool\\u0026#39;s response shape is **not described** in the README — yet it returns a rich JSON object with 9 fields (including an optional `generatedGuard` sub-object), which is equally important for agents calling the tool to understand\\n- The automatic `generatedGuard` behaviour — when a Generated/ snapshot exists, `build_and_classify` auto-verifies integrity and reverts any violations, returning the result in the `generatedGuard` field — is completely absent from the README\\n- The `classify_errors` tool\\u0026#39;s inputs and response are also undescribed; it classifies a batch of raw build errors rather than running a build itself, but there\\u0026#39;s no indication of what JSON it returns\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/tools/Azure.GeneratorAgent/README.md` — **edit**\\n\\nIn the MCP Tools table, update the `build_and_classify` row from:\\n\\n```markdown\\n| `build_and_classify` | Run `dotnet build`, parse output, and classify each error as deterministic or requires-reasoning |\\n```\\n\\nto:\\n\\n```markdown\\n| `build_and_classify` | Run `dotnet build`, parse output, and classify each error as deterministic or requires-reasoning. Returns `success`, `exitCode`, `totalErrors`, `returnedErrors` (capped at 20), `deterministicCount`, `requiresReasoningCount`, `classifiedErrors` (list of classified error objects), `rawOutput`, optional `buildFailureHint` (when errors couldn\\u0026#39;t be parsed), and optional `generatedGuard` (when a Generated/ snapshot exists — auto-verifies integrity and reverts any violations). |\\n```\\n\\nUpdate the `classify_errors` row from:\\n\\n```markdown\\n| `classify_errors` | Classify a batch of build errors against the deterministic fix registry |\\n```\\n\\nto:\\n\\n```markdown\\n| `classify_errors` | Classify a batch of pre-parsed build errors against the deterministic fix registry without running a build. Returns a list of classified error objects, each with `errorCode`, `message`, `filePath`, `line`, `isDeterministic`, `toolName`, and `toolArgs`. |\\n```\\n\\nAlso add a new sub-section under **Key concepts** → after the MCP Tools table, to document the `generatedGuard` automatic behaviour:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Generated code integrity guard\\n\\nWhen `build_and_classify` is called and a Generated/ snapshot exists (taken via `snapshot_generated`), the tool automatically verifies that no files in `Generated/` were modified during the build-fix cycle. If violations are found, they are auto-reverted and the `generatedGuard` field in the response will contain:\\n\\n- `violated` — `true` if violations were detected\\n- `message` — human-readable summary\\n- `violations` — list of `{ relativePath, type }` objects describing each modified or added file\\n\\nThis guard prevents accidental edits to generated code and runs transparently on every `build_and_classify` call after a snapshot is taken.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNo snippet test files are needed for this documentation change. 2026-04-09T02:59:47.8015407Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-09T02:58:30.647159943Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"total_count\":30,\"incomplete_results\":false,\"items\":[{\"id\":4228787321,\"number\":57967,\"state\":\"open\",\"locked\":false,\"title\":\"[Azure Management Generator] Docs: Document scope-based extension resource parameter ordering and complex parent path ValidateRe\\n[Content truncated due to length]\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt`\\n**Service directory:** `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/`\\n**Triggered by:** PR #57934 by `@live1206`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nTwo bug fixes were merged for the Azure Management SDK generator/emitter:\\n\\n1. **Generator (`OperationContext.cs`):** Fixed a LIFO stack push-order bug in `BuildContextualParameterHierarchy`. For scope-based extension resources (e.g., `ChaosTarget`, `KubernetesClusterExtension`), the stack was producing reversed positional order, causing `Id.Name` and `Id.ResourceType.Type` to be swapped in REST call arguments.\\n\\n2. **Emitter (`resource-metadata.ts`):** Fixed `getExpectedParentResourceType` to handle parent paths with more than 3 segments. For resources scoped under nested multi-provider paths (e.g., `managementGroups/{mgId}/subscriptions/{subId}/providers/Microsoft.Quota/...`), the function now falls back to computing the parent scope resource type from the last `/providers/` segment, enabling `ValidateResourceId` generation for those collections.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The `Extension Resources` section in `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/OperationContext.md` (lines 162–168) is a two-sentence placeholder. It does not explain scope-based extension resources, how the `BuildContextualParameterHierarchy` LIFO stack resolves positional parameters for them, or the interaction between `Id.Name` and `Id.ResourceType.Type` for extension resource contexts.\\n- There is no documentation describing how the emitter's `getExpectedParentResourceType` function computes the expected parent resource type for collection resources, the 3-segment rule, or the fallback to the last `/providers/` segment for multi-provider nested scopes.\\n- There is no changelog entry for either fix in the `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/` package — there is no `CHANGELOG.md` at all in that directory.\\n\\n\\u003cdetails\\u003e\\n\\u003csummary\\u003e\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\u003c/summary\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### File 1: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/OperationContext.md` (edit)\\n\\nExpand the **Extension Resources** section (currently lines ~162–168) to cover scope-based extension resources in detail. Replace the current terse section with:\\n\\n````markdown\\n### Extension Resources\\n\\nExtension resources have a flexible scope — they can be attached to any Azure resource. Their contextual path starts with a single `resourceUri` variable:\\n\\n```\\n/{resourceUri}/providers/Microsoft.Example/extensions/{extensionName}\\n```\\n\\nThe `resourceUri` parameter is treated as a contextual parameter derived from the parent resource's `Id`.\\n\\n#### Scope-Based Extension Resources\\n\\nSome extension resources also capture the parent resource **type** as a contextual parameter in addition to its name. For example, a `ChaosTarget` contextual path looks like:\\n\\n```\\n/{scope}/providers/Microsoft.Chaos/targets/{targetName}\\n````\\n\\nWhen the generator builds `ContextualParameter` objects for such resources, each variable segment in the path is paired with the constant key that precedes it using a LIFO (stack-based) algorithm. The variable pairs are pushed onto the stack in **key-then-value** order so that the resulting positional list matches the path order from outermost to innermost scope.\\n\\nThe generated REST call correctly supplies arguments in this order:\\n\\n````csharp\\n_restClient.Get(\\n    Id.Parent.ResourceType.Type,  // e.g., \\\"Microsoft.Kubernetes/connectedClusters\\\"\\n    Id.Parent.Name,               // e.g., \\\"myCluster\\\"\\n    Id.Name,                      // e.g., \\\"myTarget\\\"\\n    cancellationToken\\n);\\n```\\n\\nIf the stack push order were reversed (a previous bug), `Id.Name` and `Id.ResourceType.Type` would be swapped, producing incorrect REST calls.\\n````\\n\\n#### File 2: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/OperationContext.md` (edit)\\n\\nAdd a new subsection under **Advanced Scenarios** documenting the emitter's `ValidateResourceId` generation for complex parent paths. Add after the **Singleton Resources** subsection:\\n\\n````markdown\\n### ValidateResourceId for Complex Multi-Provider Scopes\\n\\nWhen generating a resource collection, the emitter computes the **expected parent resource type** to emit a `ValidateResourceId` guard in the `GetAll` method. For simple paths (≤ 3 segments), the parent type is derived directly from the second-to-last provider segment.\\n\\nFor nested multi-provider paths with more than 3 segments — such as:\\n\\n```\\nmanagementGroups/{mgId}/subscriptions/{subId}/providers/Microsoft.Quota/quotas\\n```\\n\\nthe emitter falls back to computing the parent scope resource type from the **last `/providers/`** segment in the path. This ensures that `ValidateResourceId` is emitted correctly for these collection types even when the parent path has complex, multi-level scope nesting.\\n````\\n\\n#### File 3: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/CHANGELOG.md` (create)\\n\\nCreate a new CHANGELOG file to track generator/emitter changes:\\n\\n```markdown\\n# Changelog\\n\\n## Unreleased\\n\\n### Bug Fixes\\n\\n- Fixed incorrect parameter ordering for scope-based extension resources (e.g., `ChaosTarget`, `KubernetesClusterExtension`). The LIFO stack in `BuildContextualParameterHierarchy` was reversed, swapping `Id.Name` and `Id.ResourceType.Type` in generated REST client arguments.\\n- Fixed `ValidateResourceId` generation for resource collections whose parent paths contain more than 3 segments (nested multi-provider scopes such as `managementGroups/{mgId}/subscriptions/{subId}/providers/Microsoft.Quota/...`). The emitter now falls back to the last `/providers/` segment to compute the expected parent resource type.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNo README snippet placeholders are needed for these documentation-only changes (the docs are in `.md` files under `docs/`, not in a package README that uses the snippet generation tooling).\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/README.md` is an npm package README (not a client library README), so the standard section checklist (Getting Started, Key Concepts, Examples, Troubleshooting, etc.) is not required. No changes needed to `README.md`.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nThe docs changes are Markdown-only; no build or test commands are required. However, if any `.cs` source was touched, run:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/`\\n2. `dotnet test eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n\\n\\u003c/details\\u003e\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u003e [!TIP]\\n\\u003e **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u003e Generated by
